# Verfix
> Browser verification runtime for AI-generated web apps

Verfix enables AI coding agents to verify real browser behavior after making UI changes.
Agents get structured JSON output with typed failure classifications and actionable fix hints.
Humans get an execution timeline with full observability.

## Install
npm install -g verfix
# or use without installing:
npx verfix init

## Quickstart
verfix init                                    # interactive setup wizard
verfix start                                   # start the Docker runtime
verfix start --show-browser                    # start with visible browser (debugging)
verfix flows                                   # list available flows
verfix run --flow <id> --output json           # run a specific flow
verfix run --flow <id> --show-browser          # run with visible browser (debugging)
verfix run --output json                       # run all flows
verfix stop                                    # stop the runtime

## Output contract
{
  "passed": boolean,
  "failures": [
    {
      "type": FailureType,
      "selector": string | undefined,
      "detail": string | undefined,
      "fix_hint": string
    }
  ],
  "timeline_url": string,
  "exit_code": 0 | 1,
  "execution_id": string
}

## Failure types (FailureType union)
- selector_not_found    — CSS/testId selector matched zero elements in DOM
- selector_not_visible  — Element exists but is hidden (display:none, visibility:hidden, zero size)
- text_mismatch         — Expected text was not found on the page
- url_mismatch          — Current URL does not contain the expected substring
- console_error         — One or more console.error() calls were detected
- network_failure       — A required network request returned non-2xx status
- timeout               — An operation exceeded the configured timeout
- assertion_failed      — Generic fallback; check fix_hint for details

## Assertion types
- page_loaded           — Page loaded without navigation error
- selector_visible      — Element is present and visible
- text_visible          — Text string appears on page
- url_contains          — Current URL contains substring
- title_contains        — Page title contains substring
- no_console_errors     — No console.error() calls during flow
- network_request_success — Network request to URL pattern returned 2xx

## When to use Verfix
After editing React components, forms, routes, buttons, or any UI code that affects user flows.

## Config file (verfix.config.json)
{
  "baseUrl": "http://localhost:3000",
  "mode": "assisted",
  "flows": [
    {
      "id": "login",
      "steps": [
        { "action": "navigate", "url": "/login" },
        { "action": "type", "selector": "[data-testid=email]", "value": "test@example.com" },
        { "action": "type", "selector": "[data-testid=password]", "value": "password123" },
        { "action": "click", "selector": "[data-testid=submit]" }
      ],
      "assertions": [
        { "type": "url_contains", "value": "/dashboard" },
        { "type": "no_console_errors" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

## Agent retry loop (pseudocode)
for attempt in range(maxRetries):
    result = run("verfix run --flow login --output json")
    if result.passed:
        break
    for failure in result.failures:
        apply_fix(failure.fix_hint)
else:
    open_timeline(result.timeline_url)  # surface to human

## Requirements
- Docker Desktop (running)
- Node.js 20.20.0+

## Hybrid Browser Mode
Verfix uses a dual browser execution model:
- **Host mode** (default on macOS/Windows): Workers run natively on the host machine for direct localhost access. Uses the slim server image (`verfix-server-slim`) with SQLite.
- **Container mode** (default on Linux): Workers run inside Docker with `--network=host`. Uses the full server image with PostgreSQL.
- Override via `VERFIX_BROWSER_MODE=host|container`.
- Use `--show-browser` flag on `start` or `run` for a visible headful browser window.

## Execution modes
- strict      — Deterministic, no AI. Best for CI/CD.
- assisted    — Strict first, semantic + AI fallback if selector breaks. Best for active development.
- exploratory — Natural language task, AI navigates. Not CI-grade.

## Runtime defaults
- Dashboard: http://localhost:3610
- API: http://localhost:3611
- Ports auto-increment if occupied (3612/3613, 3614/3615, ...)

## Docs
https://verfix.dev/docs

## Source
https://github.com/verfix-dev/verfix
